content.problemStatement:
We initially have an graph GGG with NNN nodes and 000 edges, and we will perform colouring operations on it.

In each colouring operation, we choose an array C=[C1,C2,....CN](1≤Ci≤N)C = [C_1, C_2, .... C_N] (1 \le C_i \le N)C=[C1​,C2​,....CN​](1≤Ci​≤N) and do the following :


For all (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i < j \le N1≤i<j≤N and Ci=CjC_i = C_jCi​=Cj​, if the edge (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) does not already exist in GGG, add it to the graph GGG.
We want to convert the graph GGG to given tree TTT. Find the minimum colouring operations necessary, and print one possible way to achieve it. It can be proven that it is always possible.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of nodes in GGG and TTT.
The next (N−1)(N - 1)(N−1) lines contain 222 integers each - (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) representing an edge between the nodes uuu and vvv in the required tree TTT.




Output Format
For each test case, on the first line, output KKK - the minimum number of operations necessary.


Then, output KKK lines :



in the iii-th line, output NNN integers, C1,C2,....CNC_1, C_2, .... C_NC1​,C2​,....CN​, the array CCC chosen for the iii-th operation. 1≤Ci≤N1 \le C_i \le N1≤Ci​≤N must hold.

If multiple sequences are possible, all will be accepted.
constraints: The given set of edges describes a tree
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 100010001000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : In the first colouring operation, since C1=C2C_1 = C_2C1​=C2​, we add the edge (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) to the graph. Thus, our tree is constructed.
Test Case 2 : The following are the edges added :

First Colouring Operation : C1=C2C_1 = C_2C1​=C2​ so (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) is added to the graph.
Second Colouring Operation : C2=C3C_2 = C_3C2​=C3​ so (2,3)(2, 3)(2,3) is added to the graph.

After the end of both operations, G=TG = TG=T so the condition is satisfied.
